notes: All 6 steps + prerequisite completed. 230 tests pass (227 orchestrator + 1 e2e + 2 lazy-import). Key discovery: packages with empty __init__.py (analyze, plan, source) required pre-imports in test files for @patch to find submodule attributes.
All 3 steps completed. Parametrized test_update_gate_mode_persists[pause/notify] replaces two individual tests. Fixed .get() to direct dict access for consistency. 37 tests pass.

summary: Create tests/test_render_review.py with shared fixtures: db fixture (tmp_path file-backed CatalogDB), app fixture (create_app with init_default_gates), client fixture (TestClient), _seed_narrative helper, _seed_edit_plan helper (inserts narrative + upserts edit_plan with render_path and validation_json), _seed_upload helper (inserts narrative + upload record)

Completed all remaining steps. Committed step-12 (render review page + template). Added step-13 tests for uploads page, implemented step-14 (uploads page route + uploads.html). Added step-15 hub integration tests, implemented step-16 (_REVIEW_LINKS, pending counts, render index page). All 27 tests pass. Note: steps 1-11 were done in a prior session that did not update plan.json or iterations.jsonl.

Write failing tests for CatalogDB.list_edit_plans(): returns list of all edit plans joined with narrative title; returns empty list when none exist; includes all columns (narrative_id, edl_json, otio_path, validation_json, render_path, narrative_title); Implement list_edit_plans() in CatalogDB — SELECT e.*, n.title as narrative_title FROM edit_plans e LEFT JOIN narratives n ON e.narrative_id = n.narrative_id. Returns list[dict].; Write failing tests for CatalogDB.list_uploads(): returns list of all uploads joined with narrative title; returns empty list when none exist; includes all columns (narrative_id, youtube_video_id, youtube_url, uploaded_at, privacy_status, narrative_title); Implement list_uploads() in CatalogDB — SELECT u.*, n.title as narrative_title FROM uploads u LEFT JOIN narratives n ON u.narrative_id = n.narrative_id ORDER BY u.uploaded_at DESC. Returns list[dict].; Write failing tests for GET /api/renders/{narrative_id}: returns JSON with edit plan data (narrative_id, render_path, validation parsed from validation_json, narrative title); returns 404 for nonexistent narrative_id; Implement api_get_render route in review.py — GET /api/renders/{narrative_id}. Fetches edit_plan and narrative, parses validation_json into dict, returns composite JSON response with render status. Raises 404 if no edit plan found.; Write failing tests for GET /api/renders/{narrative_id}/video: returns 200 with video/mp4 content-type when render file exists (use tmp_path to create a small mock .mp4 file); returns 404 when no edit plan exists; returns 404 when render_path is null or file missing; Implement api_stream_video route in review.py — GET /api/renders/{narrative_id}/video. Fetches edit_plan, validates render_path exists on filesystem, returns FileResponse(render_path, media_type='video/mp4'). Starlette FileResponse handles Range headers natively for seeking.; Write failing tests for GET /api/uploads: returns JSON list of uploads with narrative_title joined; returns empty list when no uploads exist; includes all fields (narrative_id, youtube_video_id, youtube_url, uploaded_at, privacy_status, narrative_title); Implement api_list_uploads route in review.py — GET /api/uploads. Calls db.list_uploads(), returns JSON list.; Write failing tests for GET /review/render/{narrative_id}: returns 200 HTML containing video player element when render_path exists and file is present; shows 'No render available' when render_path is null; shows validation results when validation_json is set; shows script scene list from narrative_scripts; returns 404 for nonexistent narrative; contains links to gate actions (approve, skip); Implement render_review_page route (GET /review/render/{narrative_id}) in review.py and create renders.html template. Route fetches narrative, edit_plan, and narrative_script; parses validation_json and script_json; passes structured context to template. Template: extends base.html, two-column layout — left side has HTML5 <video> player pointing to /api/renders/{id}/video with controls, validation results as badges/list, and action buttons (Approve for Upload, Reject with notes textarea, Download link, Skip Upload); right side has script scene list parsed from narrative_scripts.; Write failing tests for GET /review/uploads: returns 200 HTML with table containing upload data (narrative title, YouTube video ID, YouTube URL, privacy status, uploaded_at); shows 'No uploads yet' when no uploads exist; Implement uploads_page route (GET /review/uploads) in review.py and create uploads.html template. Route calls db.list_uploads() and passes to template. Template: extends base.html, simple table with columns for narrative title (linked to render review), YouTube video ID, YouTube URL (as clickable link), privacy status badge, uploaded_at timestamp.; Write failing tests for review hub integration: when render gate is 'waiting', hub page contains 'render' card with link to /review/render and pending count of narratives with edit plans but no render_path; when upload gate is 'waiting', hub contains 'upload' card with link to /review/uploads; Update _REVIEW_LINKS dict to add 'render': '/review/render' and 'upload': '/review/uploads'. In review_hub route, add render stage pending count logic (count edit plans without render_path) and upload stage logic (count narratives in rendered status without upload record). Add simple GET /review/render index route listing narratives with edit plans and links to individual render review pages.
Wrote failing test exposing upload pending count bug (counted completed uploads instead of pending). Fixed with set-difference logic: rendered_ids - uploaded_ids. All 28 tests pass.
